name: Orion Group Holdings Inc
business: Orion Group Holdings, Inc. is a leading specialty construction company focused on large-scale, mission-critical, capital projects within the marine and infrastructure sectors. It provides comprehensive engineering, construction, dredging and specialty services to federal, state and local governmental agencies as well as private commercial and industrial enterprises. Its concrete business offers turnkey concrete construction services for commercial, industrial, multi-family residential and public projects.

exposures.sentence: A rapidly growing portion of our concrete activity relates to data centers, which are critical infrastructure supporting artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and increased digital consumption.

U.S. Navy modernization efforts, particularly in the Pacific, where the Navy has outlined approximately $80 billion of planned investment under the Pacific Deterrence Initiative to upgrade shipyards, dry docks, and waterfront infrastructure.
Energy and petrochemical investment, including maintenance dredging and waterfront construction for liquefied natural gas, refining, and chemical facilities.
We install and remove underwater pipelines and transmission infrastructure, including industrial intakes and outfalls, river crossing, and tie-ins.

catalysts.event: Acquisition of J.E. McAmis, Inc. and JEM Marine Leasing, LLC
catalysts.sentence: On February 3, 2026, we entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement (the JEM Purchase Agreement ) and completed an acquisition (the JEM Acquisition ) of all of the capital stock of J.E. McAmis, Inc., a California corporation, and all of the membership interests in JEM Marine Leasing, LLC, a Washington limited liability company (collectively, JEM ).

tamClaims.claim: Federal disaster recovery funding administered by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, including approximately $7 billion allocated for projects in Texas.
tamClaims.sentence: Federal disaster recovery funding administered by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE), including approximately $7 billion allocated for projects in Texas.
